questions.q: In which year was the Hindi film Manthan released?
questions.explanation: Manthan was released in 1976.

questions.q: Who directed the 1976 film Manthan?
questions.explanation: Manthan was directed by Shyam Benegal.

questions.q: How was the 1976 film Manthan unusually financed?
options: By two-rupee contributions from roughly 500,000 Gujarat farmers
Entirely by the Indian government's film board
questions.explanation: Manthan was financed by two-rupee contributions from roughly 500,000 Gujarat dairy farmers, making it an early crowd-funded Indian feature.

questions.q: The 1976 film Manthan is set against which cooperative movement in India?
questions.explanation: Manthan dramatizes the dairy cooperative movement in rural Gujarat.

questions.q: The 1976 film Manthan was inspired by the work and ideas of which pioneer of India's dairy revolution?
questions.explanation: Manthan was inspired by Verghese Kurien, the architect of Operation Flood and India's White Revolution.

questions.q: Which large-scale dairy development programme is reflected in the story of the 1976 film Manthan?
questions.explanation: Manthan reflects Operation Flood, the dairy cooperative programme that powered India's White Revolution.

questions.q: Which actor played the idealistic veterinary doctor Dr. Rao who sets up the milk cooperative in the 1976 film Manthan?
questions.explanation: Girish Karnad played Dr. Rao, the veterinary officer who organizes the village dairy cooperative in Manthan.

questions.q: Which actress played the spirited lower-caste village woman Bindu in the 1976 film Manthan?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil played Bindu in Manthan, one of her early roles in parallel cinema.

questions.q: Which acclaimed actor played the powerful village sarpanch who opposes the milk cooperative in the 1976 film Manthan?
questions.explanation: Amrish Puri played the manipulative village sarpanch who works against the new cooperative in Manthan.

questions.q: Which actor portrayed the village dalit Bhola in the 1976 film Manthan?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah played the dalit youth Bhola in Manthan.

questions.q: Which noted playwright wrote the screenplay of the 1976 film Manthan?
questions.explanation: The screenplay of Manthan was written by the Marathi playwright Vijay Tendulkar, a frequent Shyam Benegal collaborator.

questions.q: Who composed the music for the 1976 film Manthan?
questions.explanation: Vanraj Bhatia composed the music for Manthan, as he did for several of Shyam Benegal's films.

questions.q: Preeti Sagar's well-known folk-style song from the 1976 film Manthan begins with which words?
options: Aaj phir jeene ki tamanna hai
questions.explanation: The popular Gujarati-flavoured song from Manthan, sung by Preeti Sagar, is 'Mero gaam katha parey'.

questions.q: In which Indian state is the 1976 film Manthan primarily set?
questions.explanation: Manthan is set in rural Gujarat, the heartland of India's dairy cooperative movement.

questions.q: The 1976 film Manthan won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film in which language?
questions.explanation: Manthan won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film in Hindi.

questions.q: Apart from Best Feature Film in Hindi, the 1976 film Manthan also won a National Film Award in which category?
questions.explanation: Manthan also won the National Film Award for Best Screenplay.

questions.q: The dairy cooperative depicted in the 1976 film Manthan is closely modelled on which real-life Gujarat cooperative?
questions.explanation: Manthan is modelled on the Amul / Kaira District Cooperative Milk Producers' Union in Gujarat.

questions.q: Roughly how much did each contributing farmer give to fund the 1976 film Manthan?
questions.explanation: Each of the roughly 500,000 farmers contributed two rupees to finance Manthan.

questions.q: Which organisation, the body behind the Amul brand, helped fund the 1976 film Manthan through its member farmers?
options: The Gujarat Cooperative Milk Marketing Federation
questions.explanation: Manthan was funded by farmers of the Gujarat dairy cooperatives, the network marketed under the Amul brand by the Gujarat Cooperative Milk Marketing Federation.

questions.q: The title 'Manthan' of the 1976 Shyam Benegal film translates to which English word?
questions.explanation: 'Manthan' means 'The Churning', evoking both the churning of milk and of society.

questions.q: Who served as the cinematographer for Shyam Benegal's 1976 film Manthan?
questions.explanation: Govind Nihalani was the cinematographer on Manthan, a frequent collaborator of Shyam Benegal.

questions.q: The 1976 film Manthan is often grouped with which movement in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Manthan is a landmark of parallel cinema, the realist Indian New Wave of the 1970s.

questions.q: Verghese Kurien, whose dairy work inspired the 1976 film Manthan, is popularly remembered by which nickname?
options: The Father of the Green Revolution
questions.explanation: Verghese Kurien, architect of Operation Flood and the inspiration for Manthan, is known as the 'Milkman of India'.

questions.q: Which of these films, like the 1976 Manthan, was an early-1970s feature directed by Shyam Benegal?
questions.explanation: Ankur (1974) was Shyam Benegal's debut feature, made shortly before Manthan.

questions.q: Decades later, in which year was a restored version of the 1976 film Manthan screened at the Cannes Film Festival's Classics section?
questions.explanation: A restored print of Manthan was screened in the Cannes Classics section in 2024.

questions.q: The central conflict in the 1976 film Manthan pits the new milk cooperative against which entrenched interests?
options: Private milk contractors and the village's upper-caste establishment
questions.explanation: In Manthan the cooperative struggles against private milk contractors and the entrenched caste and power structure of the village.

questions.q: The 1976 film Manthan is best described as belonging to which type of cinema?
questions.explanation: Manthan is a socially realistic art film rooted in rural Indian life.

questions.q: Which veteran character actor, later famous as 'Mogambo' in Mr. India, played the antagonist in the 1976 film Manthan?
questions.explanation: Amrish Puri, later iconic as Mogambo in Mr. India, played the antagonist opposing the cooperative in Manthan.

questions.q: Because of how it was financed, the 1976 film Manthan is frequently cited as an early example of what in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Funded by half a million farmers' small contributions, Manthan is cited as an early example of crowd-funded filmmaking in India.

questions.q: When the cooperative's farmers financed the 1976 film Manthan, those roughly 500,000 contributors effectively became the film's what?
questions.explanation: By each paying two rupees, the roughly 500,000 Gujarat farmers effectively became the producers of Manthan.
